Section 1 At the beginning
This instruction manual is the guidebook for operation of Kawasaki Heavy Industries (KHI)
incinerator.
This manual includes instruction for equipment that KHI does not supply.
In the scope of GS, GS must check this manual’s contents and revise or make it.
Important information
 KHI incinerator shall not be operated before this instruction manual is sufficiently understood with
reading it enough.
 This instruction manual shall carefully be preserved at hand for customary confirmation in case of
operation of KHI incinerator.
 Caution matters indicated in the contents of this document don’t necessarily include the whole
danger. In case of operation of KHI incinerator, therefore, sufficient consideration is necessary
for safety precaution without limiting to matters stated in this document.
 Indistinct matters in this instruction document shall be inquired of the manufacturer, i.e. Kawasaki
Heavy Industries., Ltd.

1-1 Section 2 For safety use
For security of safety
For security of safety, this instruction manual shall sufficiently be understood with enough reading
it without fail before operation and manipulation of facilities and before inspection and maintenance
of devices and equipment. In addition, this manual shall always be kept at hand to keep correct
operation, manipulation, inspection and maintenance in mind.
Fundamental caution matters are described in this chapter.
As matters of fundamental guides to be necessary for safety are described, they shall sufficiently
understood and endeavors shall be made to prevent serious damage or injury of human and material
aspects.
Manipulation other than that stated in the instruction manual shall not be executed.

1-2 WARNING Cautions for maintenance, mending and remodeling
 Working persons shall certainly wear safety devices when they come in the incinerator or the
sifting hopper for maintenance of the stoker. In addition, attention shall be paid to objects
dropping from the upper part and a safety belt shall be used to prevent falling during work.
Moreover, sufficient measures shall be taken to counter dust (wear of air-line respirators, dust
clothes and others).
 Protective devices taking account of safety, such as helmets, safety belts, safety glasses, safety
shoes, gloves, shall surely be worn during work.
 Working shall surely be executed at the non-operating time of the stoker. In the case where it is
necessary that measures are taken to prevent the stoker driving unit from moving owing to any
cause, no matter how this unit is under stopping, working shall be started after taking
countermeasures.
 Care shall be taken not to hurt the waste or others when lifting or conveying heavy matters such
as fire grates. Moreover, working shall be executed cautiously not to drop these heavy matters.
 Replacing work of fire grates shall be executed with taking enough care of finger pinching or
others.
 In the case where fire grates are replaced, care shall be taken of injury due to making a false step
or others with paying attention to steps when moving on the stoker.
 Enough care shall be taken of dropping in the vicinity of stoker front of each stoker.
 Welding work shall be executed after surely confirming that inflammable gas is completely
replaced with air.
 Dangerous working shall not be executed. In the case where dangerous working is necessary to
be executed inevitably, it shall be performed after taking measures for safety.
 Simultaneous working of the upper part and lower part of the stoker shall not be executed. In
the case where this working is necessary to be executed inevitably, it shall be performed with
taking enough care to avoid danger by means that working persons get in contact with one
another.
 Working shall not be executed with putting stepladders or others on the surface of fire grates.
This is dangerous because of instability.
 When operating in the incinerator and the stoker bottom hopper, indicate the sign of "Men
working inside" at the entrance (manhole) to avoid being locked up.

 When entering the incinerator and the stoker bottom hopper, take measurements and ventilate to
maintain oxygen concentration in the air 18% or more and locate an observer.
Otherwise, you may suffer from oxygen deficiency disease.
 When entering the incinerator and the stoker bottom hopper, make sure that the inside is cooled
off sufficiently. Otherwise, you may get burned.
WARNING Cautions for preparations before starting, starting, stopping and
steady operation
 Inspection of the stoker shall be executed according to the list of inspection items.
 Portions that anything unusual has been detected during the last operation shall certainly
repaired, and portions that have been anxious shall be confirmed so that they may not cause
trouble.
 Materials temporarily and various tools for maintenance at the non-operating time of the stoker
shall removed completely.
 Removed parts and others shall completely be returned to the former state.
 Operation of the stoker under stopping shall be started after confirming safety of the field.
 In the case where anything unusual is caused during starting, stopping and normal operation or
where it is judged that anything unusual is possible to be caused, appropriate action shall be
taken.

1-6 3-3. Basic operation
In operating incinerators, special care must be taken with the following items.
Operation safety check
Complete incineration of refuses: Complete combustion of refuses and decrease in ignition loss
of ash
Stable boiler evaporation volume
To attain these operational targets, it is necessary to perform inspection and maintenance on the
equipment on a regular basis, maintain optimum operational state, analyze and examine various
operational and measurement data to improve operating technique.
Collected from an unspecified number of households, refuse is a type of nonuniform fuel of which
composition and characteristics (water content, combustible content, ash content, heat value) will
change constantly the combustible air volume, combustion gas temperature, and boiler evaporation
volume can vary depending on dumped refuses. It is necessary, therefore, to improve operational
accuracy.
3-3-1. Refuse damping operation
In dumping refuses intermittently into the incinerator charging hopper with the refuse crane, the
following important matters must be focused on.
① Adjustment of refuse quality
In improving inequality of refuse combustion, it is of utmost importance to homogenize the
composition of the refuses dumped into the incinerator by agitating the refuses in the refuse pit.
Crane operators must have a technical intuition to distinguish non-flammable refuses from
flammable refuses through visual check.
In agitation process, the operator uses the crane bucket to grab and hoist refuses and then
release the refuses while driving the bucket over other refuses. This operation results in
homogenization of refuse heat value, permeability of refuses, and better combustion.
chunks.header: 3. As refuses start to catch fire, open the stoker bottom air damper at ignition position

chunks.text:
genization of refuse heat value, permeability of refuses, and better combustion. CAUTION
General wastes that are agitated sufficiently in the refuse pit and thus homogeneous in heat value to
the greatest possible extent must be dumped into the incinerator.
Industrial wastes must not be dumped into the incinerator.
Failure to control refuse quality causes early damage in the incinerator.
Dumping of large waste materials, excessive metal, and extremely-high-heat-generating wastes can
directly lead to damaged equipment (charging hopper, stoker).

1-7 ② Homogeneousness refuse feed
Homogenize the quality of refuses as much as possible before dumping into the refuse
charging hopper.
The refuses dumped into the hopper will move from the feeding port into the incinerator by
means of the self-weight and reciprocating movement of the pusher. The feed rate, therefore, is
subject to influence of refuse quality (apparent specific gravity and water content).
Generally, flammable refuses, mostly paper or wood chips, will not drop into the hopper
smoothly and cause bridges easily due to their small water content and apparent specific gravity.
Fire-retardant refuses, which have a high water content and apparent specific gravity, can drop
into the refuse hopper chute more smoothly due to their much water content and apparent
specific gravity..
To reduce bridges to the greatest possible extent, it is desirable that a large amount of refuses
is not dumped at a time from the bucket onto the center of the hopper and that the bucket is
opened gradually from the refuse pit side and jiggled up and down to drop refuses onto the
hopper surface just as in the manner of loosening with a hand. (Manual operation)
③ Air-tightness of refuse hopper chute
Ensure the presence of a predetermined volume of refuse in the refuse hopper chute. The
air-tightness of the refuse hopper chute is maintained by the presence of refuses. It is of
importance to feed a specific amount of refuses at regular intervals.
For moist refuses with low calorific value, it is easy to ensure air-tightness due to large
apparent specific gravity even when dumped refuse layer has decreases abnormally. On the
other hand, since the refuses with high calorific value have permeable refuse layers, an abnormal
layer decrease allows external air ingress and shifts the furnace pressure to positive pressure side,
thereby resulting in increased loading on the induced draft fan.
Cold-air ingress leads to excessive air and thus lowers the furnace temperature.
chunks.header: CAUTION
chunks.source_path: D:\기계도면\기계도면\17. 소각설비, 유압설비 (KHI, 태광)\소각 설비\17.운전 및 유지관리 지침서\FB020 OPERATION MANUAL.pdf

chunks.text:
Cold-air ingress leads to excessive air and thus lowers the furnace temperature. IMPORTANT
Maintain air-tightness in the refuse hopper chute.
At worst, the furnace pressure may turn positive, thus resulting in fire disaster or burnout.
3-3-2. Protection of stoker
In preventing the stokers from burning out, be careful with the refuse thickness not to expose
the grate surface. During operation, keep a strict eye on refuse thickness, which has a direct
effect on combustion condition.

Chapter.2 Charging hopper and refuse feeder 1. Outline
The charging hopper should be equipped with the following functions.
• Reception of refuse from the crane should be easy, a [bridge] should be caused
hardly and the refuse should be dropped smoothly.
• It is capable of storing the refuse and of shielding the furnace from outside air to
prevent ignition of refuse in the hopper.
• It has such a construction that furnace is completely isolated from outside air while
operation is stopped.
• It is protected from radiation heat from the furnace, capable of absorbing thermal
expansion and has such a construction so that an undue force may not rest on it.
• It has such a construction that ignition of refuse in the hopper may be prevented.
Further, good design considerations should be given for wear of feeder unit and
replacement of worn part.
The charging hopper of this facility is designed taking due care of construction to
satisfy functions mentioned above.
[1] Refuse receiving part from the crane has a large area (5.1 m x 6.6 m) to allow easy
reception.
[2] A nose with minimum chute area is provided at such a portion successive from
upper hopper to the middle chute.
The refuse compressed somewhat at the nose drops smoothly without causing a
bridge. To improve refuse sealing, the charging hopper is provided with a vertical
structure.
[3] An ultrasonic wave transmitter, which is provided on top space of the upper hopper,
detects refuse level in the hopper and functions as a bridge when there are no
changes in refuse level. A bridge remover is also provided to remove the bridge.

2-4 2-2 Hopper gate
A hopper gate being driven by the hydraulic cylinder is provided just underneath the
opening of the upper hopper. This provides sealing between the refuse pit and
furnace inside while the furnace is not in operation.
[1] Functions at furnace starting
The hopper gate is fully closed while the furnace is started, temperature is increased
using the auxiliary burner and preparations are done for refuse charging. Thus gas
shielding is provided between the refuse pit and furnace inside. At refuse
charging, as much as one or two bucketful refuse is placed on the hopper gate and
further, a bucket grabbed refuse is made ready on the hopper gate. Then the
hopper is opened slowly. The refuse on the hopper gate passes through the chute
and slips down to the refuse charging unit. The bucket is opened just before the
refuse on the hopper is run out to add the refuse. Even if the hopper gate is fully
opened, charging port to furnace inside is covered by the refuse thus charged while
good gas sealing is maintained all the time between the refuse pit and furnace
inside.

[2] Functions at furnace stopping
While the furnace is stopped, refuse
level in the hopper chute decreases
gradually. It is designed that the
hopper gate can be fully closed while
the charging port to furnace is closed.
This design consideration allows
complete stop of refuse charging while
good gas sealing between refuse pit and
furnace inside is maintained while the
furnace is stopped.

2-6 [3] Setting of opening/closing of hopper gate
(a) Opening/closing of hopper gate
• Opening of hopper gate
When the limit switch is kicked slightly before fully open position, the hydraulic
cylinder continues actuation for the time set by the timer. This is to press the gate
to its full stroke and to open it fully. The hydraulic cylinder stops after the
prescribed time.
• Closing of hopper gate
Fully open operations are carried out in reverse order.
• Stopping
The gate stops when the push button is detached. (This is effective for operations
from the operation panel provided in the crane room.) The gate stops at the
position when the selection switch is set to Stop. (This is effective for operations
from the local operation panel.)
(b) Timer setting
Set the timer on the central operation panel to 2 - 3 seconds.
(c) Setting of limit switch

Amount of oil at returning side from the cylinder is changed by opening/closing
the throttle valve provided in the manifold block for the gate. Set it to 30
sec/stroke for both opening and closing.
[4] Maintenance

2-8 2-3 Bridge remover
The presence of bridge near the nose of the upper hopper can lead to a clogged refuse,
resulting in burnout of the upper hopper, hopper gate, and middle chute. A device,
therefore, is provided to detect and remove bridges.
[1] A bridge is detected by the following method.
Bridge detection is performed by the ultrasonic wave transmitter. The ultrasonic
wave transmitter measures the refuse level in the upper hopper and generates an
alarm of "bridge occurrence" if the refuse level remains unchanged for a certain
period of time.

2-9 [2] Actions to be taken at bridge alarm occurrence
 Check in the crane operation room if charging interval is correct.
 At the same time, check if gas blowing out is not noticed from the hopper.
 When it is confirmed that the alarm given is not wrong and that a bridge is
caused surely, take the following procedures to remove it.
(a) Use the bridge removal device.
Actuate it twice or three times consecutively.
(b) Try to close the hopper gate twice or three times.
The bridge may be removed by applying a force pushing upwards from the
lower.

2-11 [4] Construction and driving method
Construction
This is located at hopper upper area. Four fan type blades (double construction of
inner blade and outer blade) are arranged with equal interval and are connected to
the driving shaft.
When this is not used, blades are accommodated into the blade cover outside the
hopper cover so that flowing of the refuse in the hopper is not hindered at all.
Driving method
The blades are turned by 153° by the hydraulic device through rack and pinion
mechanism. Refuses of the bridge are unfastened while being pushed upward and
drop naturally. Thus a bridge is eliminated.
The flow rate of oil return can be adjusted with the slow return check valve in the
valve stand of the Hydraulic cylinder. Perform setting for Press and Return at 30
seconds per stroke.
[5] Maintenance
[Remove of carried back refuses]
Remove the blade cover and outer blades while the furnace is stopped and remove
the carried back refuses thoroughly.
[Refilling of lubricant]
Supply the lubricating oil constantly according to the lubrication list.

[1] Refuse feeder
The feeder repeats slow pushing and stopping. These operations shorten the
interval of interruption of refuse charging resulting in uniform amount of refuse
supplying.
Feeder actuation speed is regulated by an oil amount control valve. Amount of
refuse charging is adjusted by changing the time of stopping.
It is designed that the said time of stopping is automatically controlled by the signal
coming from automatic combustion control device.

Seal
The seal portion between the lower chute and refuse feeder is provided at the upper
and under surfaces of the refuse feeder. To prevent carried back refuses in this
section, a clearance of 5 mm is provided by the installed protection material.
Since it is impossible to prevent carried- back refuses completely, wire blushes are
provided to reduce carried-back refuses as much as possible, and a refuse receiving
hopper to catch carried back refuses at the lower section.

2-16 (6) Step between supply feeder and drying stoker
A step as high as about 1.5 m is provided from the refuse feeder till the drying
stoker. This step is used effectively to maintain enough distance from the refuse
feeder and the stoker and to provide isolation between the refuse supplied and the
stoker.
When high quality refuse is burnt, ignition of refuse in the hopper can be prevented
thanks to the said configuration.
When low quality refuse is burnt, drying effect is improved.

Adjustment of refuse supplying speed is adjusted by the stop time. When you

2-22 2-4-3 Adjustment of refuse feeder stroke
Limit switches are used for stroke adjustment of the supply feeder. Limit switches are
assembled to the refuse feeder position detector that is mounted to the stand at feeder
rear.
With this device, feeder movement is replaced with wire rope movement through a
wire pulley, and up/down movement of a weight is detected by limit switches. Thus
the stroke is detected.
Cylinder stroke is replaced with 1/2 stroke by this device.
Setting of limit switches
[1] Set the limit switch for position indicator for full stroke (A - D) to 784 mm interval.
Then feeder stroke becomes 1700 mm.
(784 + 33 x 2) x 2 = 1700
[2] Set the limit switch for forward position at normal operation (C) to the point
250 mm from the limit switch for D. Then forward position becomes 500 mm.
250 x 2 = 500
Unscrew the bolts to move the limit switch. Then movement can be done easily.
[3] Set the limit switch for normal stroke (B - C) to 284 mm interval. Then feeder
stroke becomes 700 mm.
(284 + 33 x 2) x 2 = 700
Minimum interval of limit switches is 100 mm. The following relationship is
established between the stroke and limit switch.
Feeder stroke = (Distance between limit switches + 66 ) x 2

equipment (combustion stoker) and final combustion equipment (after combustion stoker).
All of drying stoker, combustion stoker and after combustion stoker are of SUN type design.

3-1 2. SUN type stoker
2-1 Construction
a) The facility has a three-stage structure consisting of SUN type stokers as shown below.
The first stage SUN type stoker, drying stoker, is mainly used for drying. The second
stage SUN type stoker, combustion stoker, is mainly used for combustion. A step is
provided between each stage, which facilitates refuse agitation. The third stage SUN
type stoker, after combustion stoker, burns out combustible contents in the refuse
gradually.
Hydraulic Cylinder
(Φ140 x 250st)
Hydraulic Cylinder
(Φ140 x 250st)
Hydraulic Cylinder
(Φ140 x 250st)
The SUN type stokers have a construction in which grates are placed on the
hydraulically-driven frame. In every stage, SUN type grates are placed on the
frame installed parallel to the refuse flow. Rows of grates are arrayed as fixed
frames and movable frames that are arranged on every other row alternately. The
facility has a structure in which only the movable frames are connected to the drive
shaft installed outside the incinerator in link mechanism and the drive shaft is
operated with a hydraulic cylinder. This prevents deviation, clogging, and
entanglement of refuses, and do combustion air feed well.
The link mechanism allows reciprocating motion of the hydraulic cylinder to
provide back-and-forth motion accompanied by slight vertical motion for the
movable frames as well as the grate surface.

after-combustion stokers with air cooled grates. 1) Water cooled grate (Drying stoker)
The drying stoker, which is subject to relatively high heat load and the
severest use conditions, is provided with water-cooled grates that can reduce
grate temperature and heat load, and improve the grate life duration.
A water-cooled grate, of which under surface is coated with heat transfer
cement that thermally-conductive is good, has a structure that allows
installation on the water-cooled pipe provided in the frame.
Grates are cooled off with grate cooling water and the combustion air
provided from underneath the stoker.
The water-cooled pipe in the movable frame has a shock-absorbing structure
with the flexible hose mounted outside the incinerator.
The grate cooling water inflows from the charging hopper side of the
water-cooled pipe mounted in the frame, turns around at the furnace front
side, and then returns to the charging hopper side. In cooling off the grates
mounted at the furnace wall side (both ends in stoker width direction), the
water inflows from the furnace front side and then flows out into the
charging hopper side.

3-7 2) Air cooled grate (Combustion  AfterCombustion stoker)
The combustion and after-combustion stokers, which have lower heat load
and abrasion in comparison with the drying stoker, are provided with air
cooled grates.
Air cooled grates are placed on the frame and cooled off with the combustion
air provided from underneath the stoker.
c) Steam injectuion construction is provided to the side grate at down stream side of the
drying stoker. This construction well prevents adhesion of clinkers.
Steam for clinker prevention
3-9 2-2 Operation of SUN type stokers
With SUN type stokers, a moving frame being driven by the hydraulic driving device is
provided every two rows and executes reciprocation motion. Then refuses on the grate are
unfastened and fed forward by reciprocation of the frame.

3-10 2-3 Driving unit
The hydraulic cylinder installed at outside-incinerator side is connected with the driving lever
outside the incinerator. Driving power from the hydraulic cylinder, therefore, is transmitted
to the driving lever. The driving lever is attached to the shaft passing through underneath
the stoker. The shaft inclines pivotably by 33 degrees. The shaft is connected with the
movable frame through the lever, which provides back-and-forth motion accompanied by
slight vertical motion for the movable frame.

3-11 2-4 Driving of SUN type stoker
With SUN type stoker, hydraulic pressure generated by the hydraulic device is conveyed to
the movable frame through link mechanism.
Continuous speed adjustment or stepless regulation is possible.
The driving device is operated automatically and continuously by the timer (ACC
automatic/manual) or operated in manual mode at local.
(1) Adjustment of time for reciprocation
a) Operation time TM
Operation time TM (sec) means the time for one reciprocation of the movable frame from
stop position, forward and backward movement and returning to original position. This
equals to the time for one reciprocation of the hydraulic cylinder.
With stoker operation timer, the said TM time is constant. However, it is possible to
change the speed by throttling the flow rate control valve shown in the hydraulic diagram.

3-12 b) Stop time TS
Stop time means the time from completion of movable frame operation (after TM sec) till
next start. This time can be adjusted freely by the stop time setting timer provided on
the central operation panel.
Don't try to adjust the timer while it is under actuation.
The timer will be used in automatic mode only.
c) Adjustment of stoker speed (Refuse feeding speed)
The stoker speed - refuse feeding speed - can be adjusted by the stop time TS. When
you need to make stoker speed (refuse feeding speed) fast, shorten the stop time TS.
When you need to make stoker speed slow, lengthen the stop time TS.
Under ACC mode, TS is set automatically by ACC.
(2) Manual operations
With SUN type stoker, manual operation can be done any time by shifting the selection
switch to (Local).
a) Set the selection switch provided on the local operation panel to (Local).
b) Manual operation selection switch
When the manual operation selection switch is set to (Front), SUN type stoker moves
forward and stops at forward end. When it should be moved backward, set the said
switch to (Rear). Then the stoker moves backward and stops at rear end. However,
hydraulic pressure is still applied. So be sure to set the operation selection switch to
(Off).
When the selection switch is shifted to (Off) while the stoker is moving forward or
backward in manual mode, SUN type stoker (hydraulic cylinder) stops at arbitrary
position.

3-13 (3) Start-up and shutdown of water-cooled grate
 Check for conduction of grate cooling water in the water-cooled pipe before igniting the
burner at incinerator start-up. If the burner is activated with the grate cooling water
unconducted while the grate and water-cooled pipe remain at a high temperature, the
equipment may be damaged.
 When the incinerator is shutdown, check that inner temperature of furnace is down
sufficiently and the drying stoker temperature is 80C or less before you stop the
conduction of grate cooling water. If the grate temperature remains high, the equipment
may be damaged as descried above.
 If the temperature of the cooling water at the water-cooled grate outlet is high during
operation, increase water quantity in the cooling line.
Check the grate cooling water level. If the water level is decreased, water leaking may
have been occurred. Locate a water leak in the piping and repair it.
If the problem will not be solved after taking the measures above or the grate cooling water
is at 90C or more, clarify the cause and repair the problem promptly.

3-14 (4) Maintenance and repairing of SUN type stoker
 Greasing of bearing
Since the bearings of the SUN type stoker are subject to high loading, ensure to lubricate
the bearings at least once a day.
 Other lubrication
Perform lubrication regularly according to the specified lubrication list.
 Clogging in grate
If ashes, gravels, or metals are melted or caught in the air sending gaps for grates and
between the frames on the movable and fixed frames, remove clogging without giving a
shock to the grates and perform maintenance regularly (when the incinerator is shutdown).
 Inspection underneath the stoker
Check for ash, aluminum, and tar accumulated on the flat section of the stoker-holding
frame and the horizontal section of the shaft, and then remove the accumulation from the
stoker bottom hopper when the incinerator is shutdown.
 Maintenance of drive section
The drive section of the movable frame shall be overhauled once a year to replace abraded
pins and bushes.
 Maintenance of water-cooled grate
In carrying out maintenance of the water-cooled grates, check the following items and
repair problems if any.
 If grate cooling water is not conducted, locate a water leak and then repair it. If
leaking is detected in the piping installed in the stoker bottom hopper and the drying
stoker during incinerator operation, ensure that the incinerator and the stoker bottom
hopper are cooled off to the fullest extent before carrying out repair work when the
incinerator is shutdown.
 Check for water leaking in the water-cooled pipe and the flexible tube in the drying
stoker when the incinerator is shutdown. If the presence of ash, aluminum, or tar is
detected on the piping and the flexible tube, remove the adhesion immediately.

4. Clinker prevention device and aluminum adhesion and accumulation prevention device
When burning higher calorific value refuses, some refuses may not move smoothly due to
uneven feeding of the refuse on the grate. Such area may become reduction combustion
atmosphere due to insufficient combustion air, and air blowing out occurs due to uneven
thickness of refuse layer resulting in very high temperature combustion at some locations. If
this is the case, clinkers are easily generated. With this device, steam is injected from the
nozzle provided close to the side wall of the drying stoker. Adjust steam amount by
checking the pressure while the root valve is opened/closed appropriately.
Steam injection pressure should normally be 0.2 MPa.

Incinerator body 1. Outline
1-1. Shape of the furnace is determined taking the following items into considerations.
(1) Prevention of clinker generation
When burning higher calorific value refuse, excessively high furnace temperature should
be prevented.
Adhesion of melted ash to furnace wall should be prevented and clinker should not be
generated.
(2) Incineration of unburnt gas in the combustion gas
Unburnt gas (carbon monoxide and others) in the combustion gas should be burnt
completely.
(3) Damage of the furnace wall should be less.
(4) Monitoring and maintenance of furnace inside should be done easily.
(5) Good air tightness should be maintained to prevent entry of outside air.
1-2 Construction of incinerator
(1) Primary components of the incinerator (furnace body brick works, stoker, stoker bottom
hopper) are supported by steel frames and beams at both sides of the incinerator.
(2) A television camera for monitoring of combustion conditions in the furnace is provided at
the furnace front side and a peep window for furnace inside monitoring is provided at
furnace front side. A maintenance manhole is provided at furnace front side .
(3) Steam atomizing construction is provided the drying stoker at the side wall. Steam is
injected from stoker lower area for suppression of clinker generation.

(1) Glass of the sightglass and television camera for furnace inside wall monitoring should be
cleaned using a waste from time to time.
(2) Be sure to check the stoker bottom hopper while the furnace is not in operation. Check
the drying stoker and combustion stoker with special care if tar or aluminum is not
adhered to the casing. If adhesion of such a material is noticed, remove it thoroughly.
(If this precaution is not observed, a fire may occur in stoker bottom hopper while the
furnace is in operation.) For inspection of stoker bottom hopper, use inspection ladder
delivered as the accessory to be used for this purpose.
(3) When entering into the furnace to check the stoker, ensure that the steam root valve for
suppression of clinker generation and aluminum accumulation is fully closed.
(4) Use safety belts and protective equipment in working a high place such as when setting
temporary scaffolding inside the furnace.

3-33 Chapter.4 General combustion control method 1. Introduction
As refuse collected in a city contains various stuff, properties of the refuse such as calorific value and specific
gravity are not uniform, which makes stable combustion difficult. Therefore, the ACC (Automatic Combustion
Control: Scope of GS) is normally utilized for stable combustion to cope with variation in properties of refuse.
However, in such cases that properties of refuse are suddenly changed, or some parameters such as quantity of
refuse to be incinerated, air flow rate are materially changed manually or the facility is subjected to any other
disturbance, stable combustion cannot be ensured even with the ACC.
This technical information describes basic points to be noted with which the user of the facility can take
countermeasures (or know how to operate) when stable combustion has become difficult to be maintained only with
the ACC and manual intervention need to be or had better be executed.
Actually combustion is such a complicated phenomenon as various factors such as quantity of refuse, combustion
speed, flow rate of vapor, air flow rate, air temperature, air distribution ratio, etc. are interactive each other.
Countermeasures taken according to the text book cannot ensure solution of the problem.
We would like to ask you to understand the actual situation and to take measures appropriate for circumstances.

chunks.source_path: D:\기계도면\기계도면\17. 소각설비, 유압설비 (KHI, 태광)\소각 설비\17.운전 및 유지관리 지침서\FB020 OPERATION MANUAL.pdf

chunks.text:
erstand the actual situation and to take measures appropriate for circumstances. 2. Optimum Operation of a Furnace
(1) Conditions inside the incinerator
Try to operate the incinerator in the way as shown below in order to stabilize combustion in the furnace.
Thickness of refuse layer: Try to maintain combustion with a thick refuse layer (thickness of the refuse layer shall
be kept constant).
Drying stoker: approximately: 800 - 1000 mm
Combustion stoker: approximately: 400 - 600 mm
After combustion stoker: approximately: 300 - 500 mm
Position of combustion-stop: around the midstream of the Combustion stoker (flames occasionally seen around the
downstream of the combustion stoker are permissible)

(The Points) 1. Try to agitate refuse in the pit and to realize stable feeding of refuse by means of a crane for refuse.
 For a power generation plant using refuse, it is important to stabilize vaporizing rate. To maintain uniformity
of refuse as a fuel and to stabilize the quantity of feeding are indispensable for the above mentioned purpose. It
is important to keep uniformity of refuse in the pit by agitation and also to maintain constancy of the feeding
quantity of refuse and interval of the feeding.
chunks.header: 1. Try to agitate refuse in the pit and to realize stable feeding of refuse by means of a crane for refuse.
chunks.source_path: D:\기계도면\기계도면\17. 소각설비, 유압설비 (KHI, 태광)\소각 설비\17.운전 및 유지관리 지침서\FB020 OPERATION MANUAL.pdf

chunks.text:
aintain constancy of the feeding
quantity of refuse and interval of the feeding. 2. Perform primary combustion in drying stoker downstream side and combustion stoker upstream side.
 Make sure that the step height portion of the drying stoker and the combustion stoker will be covered by a layer
of refuse.
 Ensure that a layer of refuse is placed on the combustion stoker where possible. (When boiler evaporation is
dropped, the shortage of refuse prevents evaporation from increasing.)
 For the after-combustion stoker, feed air to the extent which ash does not fly out to implement live charcoal
burning except when the stoker is moving. (Note: There must be kindling coal.)
 Respective stokers shall perform their intended functions as indicated in their names (drying, combustion, and

4-2 3. Manual Intervention
(1) Attention and Points to be Noted When the User is to Manually Intervene the Operation
When stable operation collapses due to any disturbance and need to be recovered by manual intervention,
important parameters required to be intervened are those related to refuse feeding device (its start and stop operation,
adjustment of the stop timer and the stroke length), each stoker (its start and stop operation, adjustment of the stop
timer), air flow rate (both primary and secondary), distribution of air under the stokers, temperature of air for
combustion, etc. Note the following preamble when the above mentioned parameters are to be controlled.
chunks.header: 3. Manual Intervention
chunks.source_path: D:\기계도면\기계도면\17. 소각설비, 유압설비 (KHI, 태광)\소각 설비\17.운전 및 유지관리 지침서\FB020 OPERATION MANUAL.pdf

chunks.text:
the following preamble when the above mentioned parameters are to be controlled. 1. The primary combustion does not change immediately and shall not be changed quickly.
Condition of the primary combustion does not change in a short time but slowly. This is the case in recovery of
the stable combustion as well as in its disturbance. This means that it will take more than one hour until change to
any parameter becomes effective.
Therefore, the user shall not change any of such parameters in a short time but change it gradually, for example
once an hour. In fact, a big change to a set value may sometime cause a quick change of combustion condition.
In such case, if the operator leaves the situation as it develops, the reaction against the change in combustion would
be so big that too intense combustion and too weak one would be repeated alternatively and the stable combustion
cannot be recovered at last in the worst case.
Therefore, when there is any symptom of a change in combustion imme
chunks.header: 1. The primary combustion does not change immediately and shall not be changed quickly.
chunks.source_path: D:\기계도면\기계도면\17. 소각설비, 유압설비 (KHI, 태광)\소각 설비\17.운전 및 유지관리 지침서\FB020 OPERATION MANUAL.pdf

chunks.text:
ediately after a change to a parameter, try
to make the range of change smaller. 2. The secondary combustion is easily changed, but the basically important thing is good primary
combustion.
Parameters giving influence of the secondary combustion are gas temperature at the outlet of the incinerator and
in the combustion chamber, concentration of CO, NOx and O . Changes to these parameters become effective

relatively quickly by adjustment of flow rate of the secondary air. However, no matter how much the secondary air
is adjusted, the stable combustion would not continue without the good primary combustion.

chunks.source_path: D:\기계도면\기계도면\17. 소각설비, 유압설비 (KHI, 태광)\소각 설비\17.운전 및 유지관리 지침서\FB020 OPERATION MANUAL.pdf

chunks.text:
d, the stable combustion would not continue without the good primary combustion. 3. Stable combustion is derived from good balance between the drying and combustion processes and more
than one way should exist to establish good combustion.
Stable combustion is the result of good balance between the drying and the combustion of refuse. Therefore, we
cannot generally specify what the best set values of the parameters are. In other words, more than one way should
exist for setting the parameters.
For instance, simply discussing the drying process only, the air flow rate and the air temperature are the
parameters that can give influence on the process. For this, the higher the flow rate and the temperature are, the
quicker the refuse is dried. It is necessary to decrease agitation of refuse by longer setting time of the stop timer for
the stokers. On the other hand, if you want to dry the refuse quicker with low air flow rate and low air temperature,
you need to increase agitation of the refuse by shorter setting time of the stop timer for the stokers.
Thus, taking up drying process only, it reveals that balance among the air flow rate, the air temperature and the
setting time of the stop timer for the stokers is decisive.

chunks.text:
perature and the
setting time of the stop timer for the stokers is decisive.
4-3 4. The most important condition for stable combustion is feeding of refuse with constant quantity.
The most important thing to stabilize combustion is continuous feeding of refuse with constant quantity.
Feeding in a single whole stroke in a short time in order to incinerate a certain quantity of refuse in a given time
instead of periodic feeding of refuse little be little would disturb combustion, and might cause hidden combustion in
the worst case. It is important to set the stop timer so that the calculated quantity to be incinerated may be realized.
(it is very important to control quantity of refuse to be fed.)
chunks.header: 4. The most important condition for stable combustion is feeding of refuse with constant quantity.
chunks.source_path: D:\기계도면\기계도면\17. 소각설비, 유압설비 (KHI, 태광)\소각 설비\17.운전 및 유지관리 지침서\FB020 OPERATION MANUAL.pdf

chunks.text:
may be realized.
(it is very important to control quantity of refuse to be fed.) 5. Setting of the stop timer for the refuse feeder and of those for the stokers may be changed separately.
The stop timer for the refuse feeder controls constant feeding of refuse and the timers for the stokers control
condition of combustion. (Strictly speaking, the stop timer for the refuse feeder gives influence on thickness of the
refuse layer.) You can understand that both are independent and you may change the settings separately.
chunks.header: 5. Setting of the stop timer for the refuse feeder and of those for the stokers may be changed separately.
chunks.source_path: D:\기계도면\기계도면\17. 소각설비, 유압설비 (KHI, 태광)\소각 설비\17.운전 및 유지관리 지침서\FB020 OPERATION MANUAL.pdf

chunks.text:
understand that both are independent and you may change the settings separately. 6. Setting of the stop timer for the stoker and temperature of air for combustion shall not be changed at the
same time.
As mentioned before, the primary combustion changes slowly. Therefore, a change to setting of the stop timer
for a stoker or temperature of air for combustion would not become effective immediately. Possibly a concurrent
change to such settings would make it impossible to find the cause when combustion is deteriorated or to decide
whether or not an executed change to the settings has exerted a good result. Therefore, as a basic principle, do not
change settings of the stop timer for a stoker and temperature of air for combustion concurrently.
chunks.header: 6. Setting of the stop timer for the stoker and temperature of air for combustion shall not be changed at the

chunks.text:
the stop timer for a stoker and temperature of air for combustion concurrently. 7. Target range of adjustment for the feeding systems such as the refuse feeder and the stoker shall be plus
or minus 5 seconds at the largest.
As too much adjustment of the feeding system, especially for the stop timer for the stoker, causes disturbance to
combustion, the range of adjustment shall be limited to  5 seconds in principle and observation of combustion
should be continued for approximately one hour without touching the adjustment.
However, you need not be bound by the rule of  5 seconds for the adjustment of the stoker for the After
combustion stoker because combustion wherein is embers combustion only.

4-4 8. Changes to settings shall be such that the effects of the changes are well considered with insight.
Keeping in mind of the fact that a change in the primary combustion is slow, adjustment may overshoot the ideal
condition depending on timing with which you resumes to the original setting from the changed one. Try to
resume the original position gradually on any sign of combustion recovery.
And it becomes necessary to take measures to promote combustion when refuse in the furnace becomes
insufficient (or there exists any sign of insufficient refuse). Then, it becomes necessary to activate the refuse feeder
in order to feed refuse in the furnace. However, the condition of the furnace at that moment is such one as
accumulated heat in the furnace has got scarce (or will become scarce afterwards even if the combustion is currently
active), it will take considerable time to recover stable combustion.
Therefore, you need to carry out such operation, when necessary, that you increase temperature of air for
combustion or flow rate of air distributed to the drying stoker in order to recover the normal combustion quickly.
However, you should not forget that the settings shall be finally returned to their original ones.
chunks.header: 8. Changes to settings shall be such that the effects of the changes are well considered with insight.

monitor camera monitoring the inside of the furnace or from the measured values.
Though combustion does not appear to be good, the combustion is sometime ideal when watched through the
observation window. Or on the contrary, though water vapor is noticed through the observation window when
combustion is degrading, the monitor does not show the same image (not noticeable without gazing).

Examples of major measures for recovery of the stable combustion are described below. But, you should work
on the parameters to resume the original values in accordance with the situation on a detected sign of recovery.
The measure is not always only one. You should grasp the correct condition of the current operation from time
to time and try to choose measures appropriate for adaptation to circumstances. It is also important to try to
recover the stable combustion in the early stoker with depth of insight instead of taking measures after the
combustion condition has been worsened.

4-13 5. Others
As stated above, to recover stable combustion, you shall operate the stopping timer of the refuse feeding
equipment and the stokers and adjust air volume, distribution, and temperature. You may also adjust the refuse
heat value set value to stabilize combustion if some disturbances result in an unrealistic value, thus leading to
imbalance in the furnace. The altered set values must be restored to the automatic mode after combustion is stable.
Refuse heat value set value: a conveniently-corrected-value that is essential for calculating basic set values as
with incineration amount set value, and calculated through estimated computing of
furnace energy input and output.
chunks.header: 5. Others
chunks.source_path: D:\기계도면\기계도면\17. 소각설비, 유압설비 (KHI, 태광)\소각 설비\17.운전 및 유지관리 지침서\FB020 OPERATION MANUAL.pdf

chunks.text:
, and calculated through estimated computing of
furnace energy input and output. 6. Conclusion
Properties of refuse fed to the furnace are varying day to day and hour to hour depending on the season and the
day of the week when the refuse is collected as well as on the way of agitation in the refuse pit. In principle, we
cannot uniformly specify each setting and operation method giving influence on condition of combustion. We
would expect you to choose the best settings and operation method making use of the information describe in this
technical information.
We would also request you to carry out the maintenance without fail so that you can use the facility for a long
time.
- That is the end -
